headlines.headline: IDF Chief Condemns Unethical Soldier Conduct as Discipline Erodes
headlines.summary: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir warned that discipline has eroded in recent wars, citing incidents including soldiers smashing a Jesus statue in Lebanon and wearing unapproved patches.

headlines.headline: China Blocks Meta From Acquiring AI Startup Manus
headlines.summary: Beijing tightened scrutiny of AI industry amid intensifying geopolitical rivalry with the US over technology, moving to block Meta from acquiring the AI startup.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah and Israel Exchange Threats and Strikes on Lebanon Border
headlines.summary: Israel threatened to "burn" all of Lebanon after Hezbollah reiterated its defiance and rejection of peace talks, escalating tensions along the border.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Completes Major US Tank Deal With Final Abrams Shipment
headlines.summary: The acquisition marks the biggest upgrade to Taiwan armored forces in decades as it bolsters northern defense capabilities against Chinese threats.

headlines.headline: Global Military Spending Hits Record Nearly $2.9 Trillion
headlines.summary: Military spending across the globe reached a record high of nearly $2.9 trillion in 2025, marking the 11th consecutive year of growth driven by European NATO members and Asia.

headlines.headline: Iran ceasefire negotiations stall as both sides dig in on demands
headlines.summary: Diplomatic efforts to end the Iran war have stalled over the weekend, with both Iran and the US maintaining hardline positions. The stalemate comes two months into the conflict that has disrupted global oil supplies and heightened regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Conflict Stuck in No War No Peace Limbo as Both Sides Wait
perspectives.summary: With peace talks stalled and neither side willing to escalate further, the US-Iran standoff has settled into an uneasy stalemate where each is betting it can outlast the other.

headlines.headline: Iran war economic strain spreads across emerging markets
headlines.summary: Two months into the Iran war, emerging and developing economies are feeling the economic toll through rising inflation, fiscal strains, and trade disruptions. The conflict has extended its reach beyond the Middle East, affecting markets across Asia, Africa, and Latin America.

perspectives.angle: Iran War Creates Economic Strain Across Emerging Markets After Two Months
perspectives.summary: Two months since the outbreak of the Iran war, economic stress is spreading beyond the Middle East as emerging and developing markets face rising inflation, growing fiscal pressures, and widespread trade disruptions.

headlines.headline: Iran war may accelerate international talks to exit fossil fuels
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has disrupted oil and gas supplies and sent prices spiking, potentially invigorating discussions at IMO and Santa Marta meetings on accelerating the energy transition. Countries worldwide are reconsidering their approach to energy security.

headlines.headline: Asia Could Emerge as Unexpected Winner from Iran War Fallout
headlines.summary: Despite being heavily reliant on Middle East energy, Asia may benefit long-term from accelerated cybersecurity investment, fossil fuel diversification, and supply chain restructuring prompted by the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Asia May Emerge as Unexpected Beneficiary from Iran War Fallout
perspectives.summary: While Asia has been highly vulnerable to the Iran war due to its dependence on Middle East energy, analysts suggest the region could emerge as a long-term winner from trends accelerated by the conflict, including supply chain diversification and energy transition.

headlines.headline: Putin Hosts Iranian FM Araghchi, Pledges Support as US-Iran Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin hosted Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Moscow, promising support as Iran faces pressure over the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The meeting comes amid failed US-Iran negotiations over ending the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Putin pledges support for Iran, offers mediation in talks with Araghchi
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin told Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg that Russia would support Iran through its "difficult period" and hoped for peace soon. The meeting comes as US-Iran talks stall.

headlines.headline: North Korea and Russia Deepen Alliance at Memorial for Fallen Soldiers in Ukraine
headlines.summary: At a ceremony honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting in Ukraine, Moscow offered to renew a military pact with Pyongyang, strengthening the anti-West coalition as the war drags on.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Disrupts Russian-Linked Network Planning Assassinations and Sabotage Across EU
headlines.summary: Prosecutors announced an international operation disrupted a network preparing killings, arson, and infrastructure attacks across multiple European countries, linked to Russian intelligence.

headlines.headline: Shooting Suspect Cole Allen Charged With Attempting to Assassinate Trump
headlines.summary: The 31-year-old suspect in the White House Correspondents Dinner shooting appeared in court charged with attempted assassination of President Trump, who blamed political rhetoric for the attack.

headlines.headline: US Army Developing Heavier Hybrid-Powered Infantry Squad Vehicle
headlines.summary: The Army is testing a new hybrid-powered ISV-H vehicle designed to carry more armor and equipment while maintaining mobility for infantry squads in rugged terrain.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president cancels Africa visit after China pressures three nations to deny flight permits
headlines.summary: President Lai Ching-te postponed his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar withdrew flight permissions under Chinese pressure, in what Taiwan calls blatant interference in other countries affairs.

headlines.headline: US military seizes another Iranian oil tanker in Indian Ocean standoff
headlines.summary: The Pentagon seized the Guyana-flagged tanker Majestic X in the Indian Ocean, the latest move in the escalating maritime conflict with Iran that has effectively choked off oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Chinas Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of Trump summit
headlines.summary: Xi Jinping held a rare meeting with Kuomintang leader Cheng Li-wun in Beijing, calling for peaceful cross-strait development ahead of a May summit with Trump where Taiwan arms sales are expected to be a key topic.

headlines.headline: Worker killed at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant in Ukrainian drone attack
headlines.summary: An employee of the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant was killed in a Ukrainian drone attack, according to the Russian-installed management of the station.

perspectives.angle: Russia attacks Odesa with drones, claims Ukraine hit Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant
perspectives.summary: Russian drone attacks on Odesa injured 11 people including children, while a Ukrainian drone strike killed a worker at the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant, escalating tensions around Europes largest nuclear facility.

headlines.headline: Iran war drives fertiliser prices up, threatening global grain harvests
headlines.summary: Farmers worldwide face a second surge in fertiliser prices in four years due to the Iran war, with grain prices too low to cushion the blow from the supply crunch, putting global food production at risk.

perspectives.angle: Fertiliser Price Surge Threatens Global Grain Harvests
perspectives.summary: Farmers worldwide face a second surge in fertiliser prices in four years due to the Iran war. With grain prices too low to cushion the blow from the deeper supply crunch, many are rethinking planting plans, putting global food production at risk.

headlines.headline: Germany plans to build Europe's strongest army, says defence editor
headlines.summary: The Economist examines Germany's ambitious plan to build Europe's strongest military, analyzing whether the country can achieve its goals and what obstacles stand in the way.

headlines.headline: North Korea's Kim pledges continued support for Russia's policies
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un said North Korea would continue supporting Russia's policies and discussed bolstering military ties with Moscow, signaling cooperation will endure beyond the Ukraine war.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Opens Memorial Museum for Soldiers Killed in Russia-Ukraine War
perspectives.summary: Pyongyang unveiled a memorial honoring North Korean troops killed fighting for Russia in Kursk. Kim Jong Un pledged continued support for Moscow's "sacred war" against Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Kim Jong Un Vows Continued Support for Russia at Memorial Ceremony
perspectives.summary: At the opening of a memorial for North Korean soldiers killed in Kursk, Kim Jong Un reaffirmed support for Russia's invasion of Ukraine and vowed to help Moscow achieve victory.

headlines.headline: Congo creates US-UAE backed paramilitary guard to secure critical minerals
headlines.summary: Congo announced a $100 million paramilitary mining guard backed by US and UAE funding to secure its vast mining operations, as Washington seeks access to critical minerals like coltan amid eastern conflict.

headlines.headline: Poland's Sikorski proposes EU-funded rapid reaction force for regional crises
headlines.summary: Poland's foreign minister calls for a European rapid reaction force funded by the EU to handle neighborhood security operations, arguing Europe must become a more credible ally amid growing geopolitical risks.

headlines.headline: Lithuania charges 13 people with attempted murders linked to Russian GRU
headlines.summary: Lithuania has charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius connected to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in allegations of Russian intelligence operations on NATO territory.

headlines.headline: Google employees petition CEO to refuse classified AI work with Pentagon
headlines.summary: Hundreds of Google AI researchers have signed a letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai to refuse classified military AI work, reigniting debates about tech company involvement in defense projects.

headlines.headline: Ukraine warns Israel of diplomatic crisis over ship carrying alleged stolen grain
headlines.summary: Ukraine has warned of a potential diplomatic and legal crisis with Israel if the vessel Panormitis, carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukraine, docks at Haifa port.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Mortar Attacks Kill Four in Afghanistan, Taliban Says
headlines.summary: Mortar and rocket attacks launched by Pakistan against Afghanistan killed at least four people and injured 70 more, according to the Taliban government. The strikes mark another escalation in cross-border tensions between the neighbors.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan-Afghanistan border clashes kill 4, wound 70
perspectives.summary: Mortar and rocket attacks by Pakistan against Afghanistan killed four people and injured 70 on Monday, the Taliban government said. Cross-border violence has escalated amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Water Dispute Kills 42 in Eastern Chad as Conflict Escalates
headlines.summary: At least 42 people were killed in eastern Chad after a dispute between two families over a water well escalated into a cycle of reprisal attacks. The government deployed the military to contain the violence as resource conflicts intensify.

headlines.headline: South Sudan Plane Crash Kills All 14 On Board Near Juba
headlines.summary: A Cessna aircraft crashed southwest of South Sudan's capital Juba, killing all 14 people on board. The country's civil aviation authority said the crash occurred during adverse weather conditions.

headlines.headline: Colombia Offers Record $1.4M Reward for Rebel Leader Behind Deadly Bombing
headlines.summary: Colombia's government announced a record reward for information leading to the capture of a rebel leader known as "Marlon," accused of orchestrating a bus bombing that killed 20 people. The attack marks escalating pre-election violence.

headlines.headline: OpenAI Ends Microsoft Exclusivity, Opens Path to Amazon and Google Cloud Deals
headlines.summary: Microsoft loses exclusive access to OpenAI technology, allowing the ChatGPT creator to sell products across rival cloud platforms in a major shift for the AI industry.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strikes Hit East Lebanon, Expanding Conflict Zone Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: The Israeli military begins strikes in eastern Lebanon, expanding the scope of its bombing campaign during a ceasefire that has failed to halt hostilities with Hezbollah.

perspectives.angle: Israel warns Lebanon buffer zone evacuations after strikes kill 14
perspectives.summary: Israeli strikes killed 14 people in Lebanon, and the military warned residents to leave towns beyond the Litani River buffer zone, accusing Hezbollah of ceasefire violations.

headlines.headline: Iran Oil Tankers Forced Back by US Blockade as Hormuz Traffic Dwindles
headlines.summary: Six tankers loaded with Iranian oil were forced to return to Iran by the US naval blockade in recent days, with ship-tracking data showing only minimal traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway.

perspectives.angle: Iran Offers to Reopen Strait of Hormuz in Exchange for US Lifting Blockade
perspectives.summary: Iran has proposed reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz if the US ends its naval blockade and halts the ongoing conflict. The offer comes as diplomatic efforts intensify amid concerns over global energy supply disruption.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Norway to jointly produce mid-strike drones
headlines.summary: Ukraine and Norway will launch joint production of mid-range strike drones following a defense declaration signed in Oslo. The project expands Ukraine's drone manufacturing capabilities amid the ongoing war.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine unveils new AI-powered strike drones Anubis and Seth-X for deep missions
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian firm Airlogix revealed two new drones: Anubis for deep-strike missions with stealth characteristics, and Seth-X designed for swarm attacks, expanding Ukraine's AI strike capabilities in contested environments.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor Merz Says Iran Humiliating US in Stalled Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the lack of progress in US-Iran negotiations, saying Tehran is making American officials travel to Pakistan and leave without results while the Iranian leadership continues to dictate terms.

headlines.headline: Iran Suspends Steel Exports Through May Amid War-Related Strikes
headlines.summary: Iran has banned exports of steel slabs and sheets until May 30, as the country's steel industry faces ongoing strikes during the conflict with Israel and the United States.

headlines.headline: Mediators Continue Push to Bridge US-Iran Gaps After Failed Face-to-Face Talks
headlines.summary: Pakistan-based mediators are still working to bridge gaps between the US and Iran despite the failure of direct diplomacy after Trump called off his envoys' trip over the weekend.

perspectives.angle: Iran FM blames US for failed talks after arriving in Russia
perspectives.summary: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed Washington excessive demands for the collapse of Islamabad negotiations, arriving in Saint Petersburg to meet Putin. Iran passed written red lines to Americans via Pakistan including nuclear and Hormuz issues.

headlines.headline: Record Government Debt to Persist as Military Spending Rises Globally
headlines.summary: With government debt at historic highs across developed nations and rising military spending needs, fiscal watchdogs are being warned that calls for fiscal discipline will likely go unheeded.

headlines.headline: Israel Withholds Palestinian Clearance Funds, Deducts $200 Million
headlines.summary: Israel has decided to withhold clearance funds to the Palestinian Authority and deducted approximately $200 million this month, amid ongoing tensions over governance and security in the occupied territories.

headlines.headline: Germany's Merz Says US Has 'No Exit Strategy' in Iran War
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the US for lacking an exit strategy in the Iran conflict, calling Tehran a "skilled negotiator" as diplomatic efforts remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to Help Poland Build Modern Drone Fleet Against Aerial Threats
headlines.summary: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced Ukraine will assist Poland in building a "modern drone armada," citing Kyivs proven expertise in defending against aerial attacks.

headlines.headline: Palestinians Use Gaza Rubble to Restore Streets as US Rebuilding Plan Stalls
headlines.summary: Palestinians are crushing concrete and reusing war rubble to repave streets destroyed during Israels two-year assault on Gaza, as US-backed reconstruction efforts remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Attacks on Politicians
headlines.summary: German government suspects Russia is behind phishing attacks on Signal accounts targeting high-ranking politicians, including two ministers, military personnel and journalists. Around 300 accounts in the political sphere were compromised through fake security chatbot messages.

headlines.headline: China Expands Economic Toolkit During Trade Truce With US
headlines.summary: China has used its trade truce with the United States to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain and critical technology controls, and expand its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a summit between the nations next month.

perspectives.angle: China Uses Trade Truce With US to Expand Economic Leverage Before Summit
perspectives.summary: Beijing has used the trade truce with Washington to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain controls, and critical technology restrictions, expanding its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a potential summit between the nations next month.

headlines.headline: Merz Suggests Ukraine May Accept Territorial Loss for EU Path
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Ukraine may need to accept that some territory remains outside Kyiv's control in a future peace deal with Russia, linking such concessions to EU membership prospects.

headlines.headline: Iran Submits Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz Without Nuclear Deal
headlines.summary: Iran handed the US a proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, postponing nuclear negotiations for later. The move comes as US-Iran talks remain stalled.

perspectives.angle: Iran Offers Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz While Deferring Nuclear Talks
perspectives.summary: Iran has proposed a new deal to the US through Pakistani mediators that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for postponing nuclear negotiations to a later phase. The diplomatic outreach comes as Iranian FM Araghchi meets with Putin in St. Petersburg amid stalled US-Iran peace talks.

perspectives.angle: Iran Submits Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Iran has submitted a new proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, postponing nuclear negotiations for a later stage, Axios reported citing US officials.

headlines.headline: Moody's Revises China Outlook to Stable Citing Economic Resilience
headlines.summary: Credit ratings agency Moody's upgraded China's outlook from negative to stable, pointing to resilient economic growth and effective debt management despite ongoing trade and geopolitical challenges.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu's Rivals Bennett and Lapid Join Forces for Israeli Election
headlines.summary: Two of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's main political opponents, former PMs Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid, announced they are merging parties to challenge his coalition in elections expected later this year.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Rivals Unite to Challenge Israeli PM in Upcoming Election
perspectives.summary: Two of Netanyahu's most formidable rivals announced they are joining forces to try to oust his coalition government in elections expected later this year.

headlines.headline: Starmer and Trump Discuss Urgent Need to Restore Hormuz Shipping
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer and US President Donald Trump discussed the urgent need to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz in a call, as the closure continues to disrupt global oil supplies.

headlines.headline: Israel Sent Iron Dome System and Troops to UAE During Iran War
headlines.summary: Israel deployed an Iron Dome air defence system along with troops to operate it in the UAE during the recent conflict with Iran, marking unprecedented security cooperation between Israel and Gulf states.

perspectives.angle: Israel sent Iron Dome and troops to UAE during Iran war
perspectives.summary: Axios reports Israel deployed an Iron Dome battery and personnel to the UAE amid the Iran conflict, deepening defense ties between the countries.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Chief Rejects Direct Lebanon-Israel Talks, Outlines 5-Point Solution
headlines.summary: Hezbollah Secretary-General Naim Qassem categorically rejected direct negotiations between Lebanon and Israel, proposing a five-point framework for resolving border tensions instead.

headlines.headline: German Defense Spending Drives European Military Budgets to Decades-High Levels
headlines.summary: Military spending in Europe increased at its fastest pace in nearly 75 years in 2025, largely driven by German rearmament efforts amid the ongoing security crisis.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Receives Final Batch of M1A2T Abrams Tanks From US
headlines.summary: Taiwan has received the last of its 108 M1A2T Abrams tanks from the United States, completing a .28 billion procurement deal that strengthens the island’s armored capabilities.

headlines.headline: US Emerges as World's Swing Oil Producer as Iran War Reshapes Energy Markets
headlines.summary: The Iran war has handed OPEC's swing producer crown to the United States, which has stepped in to shield the global economy from oil supply disruptions by boosting exports and selectively easing sanctions. The conflict is cementing America's transformation into the world's dominant energy superpower.

perspectives.angle: Europe Pays €27 Billion More for Energy Due to Iran War
perspectives.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen stated Europe is facing its second major energy crisis, paying an additional €27 billion due to disruptions from the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Gulf Stock Markets Edge Higher Despite Stalled Iran Diplomacy
headlines.summary: Most Gulf equity markets eked out small gains despite fading hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict. Hardening positions on both sides underscore the difficulty of reaching a negotiated settlement.

headlines.headline: China blocks Meta acquisition of AI startup Manus
headlines.summary: Beijing has prohibited Meta Platforms USD 2 billion acquisition of Chinese-founded agentic AI startup Manus after a national security review by the National Development and Reform Commission, blocking the deal on tech transfer grounds.

perspectives.angle: China blocks Meta acquisition of Singapore AI startup Manus
perspectives.summary: China blocked Meta Platforms from acquiring AI start-up Manus, a Singapore-based company, citing national security concerns. The .5 billion deal would have been one of the largest AI acquisitions.

headlines.headline: US sanctions hit Chinese refiners linked to Iranian oil trade
headlines.summary: US sanctions against Hengli Petrochemical over alleged Iranian crude purchases triggered a sharp 10% stock drop. China largest independent refiner denied the allegations, saying it has never engaged in any trade with Iran.

headlines.headline: China delivers record LNG carrier challenging South Korean shipbuilding dominance
headlines.summary: China Merchants Heavy Industry delivered the 180,000-cubic-meter Celsius Georgetown, marking the fifth Chinese yard capable of building advanced LNG carriers as Beijing intensifies competition with Seoul in high-value shipbuilding.

headlines.headline: Mali defense minister killed in insurgent assault
headlines.summary: Mali's Defense Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks by insurgents including al Qaeda's West Africa affiliate, marking a major blow to the government.

perspectives.angle: Mali Defence Minister Killed in Car Bomb Attack
perspectives.summary: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara and family members were killed in a car bomb attack as Tuareg separatists and al-Qaeda affiliate launch a string of coordinated attacks across the country.

headlines.headline: DeepSeek releases new AI model to muted market response
headlines.summary: Chinese startup DeepSeek previewed its next-generation AI model, but market reaction was subdued compared to last year's breakthrough. The fast-moving AI industry continues to evolve rapidly.

headlines.headline: At least 42 killed in Chad water resource clashes
headlines.summary: A clash between two families over a water point in eastern Chad escalated into reprisal attacks, killing at least 42 people amid growing resource scarcity in the Sahel region.

headlines.headline: Iranian Foreign Minister Heads to Moscow as Trump Says Call Us
headlines.summary: Iran's top diplomat Abbas Araghchi arrived in Russia seeking support from Putin as President Trump said Iran could simply call if it wants to negotiate an end to the two-month war.

perspectives.angle: Trump Urges Iran to Call US Directly as Putin Hosts Iranian FM in St. Petersburg
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump publicly urged Iran to call the United States directly to restart negotiations, while Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with Russian President Vladimir Putin in St. Petersburg amid stalled diplomatic efforts.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Strike Wounds 10 Including Children in Odesa
headlines.summary: An overnight Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern port city of Odesa wounded 10 people including two children and damaged residential buildings. The strike comes amid continued fighting.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Attack Wounds 14 in Odesa, Ukrainian Strikes Kill 2 in Kherson
perspectives.summary: A Russian drone strike injured 14 civilians in Odesa while Ukrainian drone attacks killed two people in Russian-occupied Kherson, as President Zelensky reported Russia has fired approximately 1,900 attack drones in recent operations.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Apartment Building in Yekaterinburg
perspectives.summary: A Ukrainian drone hit a multi-story apartment building in Russia's fourth-largest city of Yekaterinburg, marking the first attack on the city since the conflict began. Regional authorities reported damage to residential infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Russia Launches Major Missile and Drone Attack on Multiple Ukrainian Cities, Injuring 14
perspectives.summary: Russia struck multiple Ukrainian cities overnight with missiles and drones, injuring at least 14 people. The large-scale attack continues the pattern of systematic strikes on Ukrainian civilian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Global Military Spending Surges Despite US Ukraine Aid Freeze
headlines.summary: Global military expenditure rose 2.9% in 2025, driven by defense spending increases in Europe and Asia, even as US military aid to Ukraine was frozen. The trend reflects growing security concerns worldwide.

perspectives.angle: Global Military Spending Hits Record $2.9 Trillion as Europe Surges
perspectives.summary: Global military spending reached a new record of almost $2.9 trillion in 2025, the 11th consecutive year of growth. European expenditure surged 14% to $864 billion - the highest level ever recorded for the continent, with Germany crossing 2% of GDP for the first time since 1990.

headlines.headline: China Slams EU Industrial Policy as Discriminatory, Mulls Countermeasures
headlines.summary: Beijing has sharply criticized the EU proposed industrial law, calling it a serious barrier and institutional discrimination against Chinese firms. China is considering retaliatory measures in response.

headlines.headline: North Korea and Russia Deepen Military Alliance at Troop Memorial Ceremony
headlines.summary: At a memorial for North Korean soldiers killed fighting in Ukraine, Moscow offered to renew its military pact with Pyongyang. Kim Jong Un and Russian Defense Minister Belousov unveiled the monument together.

headlines.headline: Indian Startup Develops Satellite-Based Missile Launch Detection System
headlines.summary: An Indian firm is developing space-based thermal observation systems designed to detect missile launches in hotspots, representing a significant advance in indigenous defense technology.

headlines.headline: Seven Dead in Major Russian Attack on Ukraine
headlines.summary: At least seven people were killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. The attack involved over 600 drones and missiles, one of the largest in days.

perspectives.angle: Seven Dead as Russian Strike Hits Residential Building in Dnipro
perspectives.summary: A major Russian attack on Ukraine killed at least seven people, with the city of Dnipro hardest hit after a strike on a residential building. Four deaths were reported from the Dnipro attack alone.

headlines.headline: Five Pro-Palestine Activists Face Trial for Elbit Factory Raid in Germany
headlines.summary: The trial of five European activists accused of attacking a factory linked to Israeli weapons manufacturer Elbit Systems begins in Germany. Prosecutors seek lengthy sentences, calling it a criminal organization act.

headlines.headline: Israelis Question Netanyahu Leadership as Iran War Goals Unfulfilled
headlines.summary: With Iran's government still standing and Hezbollah and Hamas not defeated, many Israelis express dissatisfaction with Netanyahu's wartime leadership ahead of elections scheduled for later this year.

headlines.headline: Can King Charles help heal the US-British rupture?
headlines.summary: Not since Queen Elizabeth II traveled to Washington after the Suez Crisis has a British monarch's visit come at such a fraught time in Anglo-American relations, with the King seeking to restore strained ties.

headlines.headline: 20,000 Seafarers Stranded in Persian Gulf as Hormuz Remains Blocked
headlines.summary: Approximately 20,000 seafarers on hundreds of vessels remain stranded in the Persian Gulf, unable to cross the effectively closed Strait of Hormuz. The UN reports at least 10 seafarers have been killed since the conflict began.

headlines.headline: Four Candidates Vie to Succeed UN Secretary-General Guterres
headlines.summary: Michelle Bachelet (Chile), Rafael Grossi (Argentina), Rebeca Grynspan (Costa Rica), and Macky Sall (Senegal) presented their visions to lead the UN, focusing on restoring global peace and reforming the institution.

headlines.headline: Iran War Taking Toll on Chinese Manufacturing Economy
headlines.summary: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on Chinese factory orders, costs and jobs as the Strait of Hormuz disruption hits exports. Costs have risen around 20% for some traders, adding strain to an economy already dealing with US tariffs and slower growth.

headlines.headline: Erdogan Offers Support to Trump After White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
headlines.summary: Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan called President Donald Trump to offer support following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents Association dinner. A gunman opened fire near the event attended by Trump and senior officials.

headlines.headline: Chinese EVs surge in South Korea with 33% market share as fuel prices climb
headlines.summary: China-made electric vehicles now account for one in three new registrations in South Korea, up from 4.7% in 2022, driven by Tesla Shanghai models and competitive pricing amid elevated fuel costs linked to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan After Iranian FM Departs
headlines.summary: President Trump canceled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushners planned trip to Islamabad for Iran peace talks, suggesting negotiations continue by phone instead.

headlines.headline: King Charles State Visit to US to Proceed Despite Washington Shooting
headlines.summary: Buckingham Palace confirmed King Charles and Queen Camilla's four-day state visit to the United States will proceed as planned starting Monday, following discussions with US officials after a shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner attended by President Trump.

perspectives.angle: King Charles III arrives in Washington for delicate state visit
perspectives.summary: King Charles III has begun a four-day state visit to the US aimed at celebrating America's 250th anniversary while attempting to repair strained UK-US relations. The visit comes amid heightened security concerns and political tensions over the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Security Under Review After Gunman Breaches White House Correspondents Dinner
headlines.summary: Law enforcement officials are reassessing security protocols after an armed suspect was able to get close to an event attended by President Trump and senior officials, raising questions about protection arrangements.

perspectives.angle: White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting Suspect Appears in Federal Court
perspectives.summary: The suspect who opened fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where President Trump and top officials were present, appeared in federal court. Authorities believe Trump and officials were likely targets of the attack.

perspectives.angle: Suspect in Press Dinner Shooting Wrote Anti-Christian Manifesto
perspectives.summary: The suspect in the White House Correspondents Dinner shooting, identified as Cole Allen, wrote an anti-Christian manifesto according to President Trump. The 31-year-old from California was armed with a shotgun.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo and the Shift From Just War to Peace Activism
headlines.summary: The popes criticism of the Iran war reflects a broader transformation in Catholic teaching on warfare, driven by the devastating power of modern weaponry and the collapse of traditional just war theory.

headlines.headline: Iranian Foreign Minister Returns to Pakistan, Seeks to Revive Ceasefire Talks
headlines.summary: Iran's Abbas Araghchi made a brief return to Islamabad as Pakistani leaders scrambled to reignite negotiations. The diplomatic push comes amid continued hostilities and a stalled Strait of Hormuz blockade.

perspectives.angle: Trump Meets Team to Discuss Iranian Proposal as Foreign Minister Visits Putin
perspectives.summary: The US President is reviewing a new Iranian proposal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Irans Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is in Moscow seeking Russian support amid stalled negotiations.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Climb Nearly 2% as US-Iran Peace Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Crude oil extended gains as diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran faltered while shipments through the Strait of Hormuz remained limited, tightening global supplies.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Surge 44% Since Iran War Began as Hormuz Transit Remains Blocked
perspectives.summary: West Texas Intermediate reached $96.50/barrel and Brent crude $107.75, up 44-48% since the Iran war began, as ceasefire talks stall and tankers remain stranded in the Persian Gulf.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels Pakistan Peace Mission, Says Iran Can Call Instead
headlines.summary: President Trump called off a planned U.S. envoy trip to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, saying Tehran can telephone if it wants to negotiate. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi headed to Moscow as diplomatic efforts stall.

perspectives.angle: Trump Tells Iran to Call If It Wants to Negotiate as Peace Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: President Trump said Iran can reach out to the US if it wants to negotiate an end to the war, adding that US negotiators will no longer travel for talks. Both Tehran and Washington have shown little willingness to soften their terms.

headlines.headline: NATO Says No Provision to Expel Members After Pentagon Email on Spain
headlines.summary: NATO responded to reports of a Pentagon email suggesting options to suspend Spain from the alliance over perceived lack of support for Iran operations, stating there is no mechanism to expel members.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Plans T+1 Settlement Shift to Sharpen Financial Hub Edge
headlines.summary: After more than 30 years of T+2 settlement, Hong Kong plans to move to T+1 settlement. The shift aims to strengthen Hong Kong competitive position as a global financial hub amid intensifying regional competition.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery Over Iranian Oil Purchases
headlines.summary: The Trump administration sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical's Dalian facility, one of China's largest independent refineries, along with 40 shipping companies for transporting Iranian oil. The move is part of a "maximum pressure" campaign to cut off Iran's key revenue source ahead of Trump's planned meeting with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: US sanctions China-based oil refinery in sweeping crackdown on Iranian oil trade
perspectives.summary: Treasury targets China teapot refinery and 40 shipping entities for purchasing Iranian oil, expanding enforcement against Tehrans shadow trade network amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Email Floats Suspending Spain From NATO Over Iran Stance
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies who failed to support US operations against Iran, including suspending Spain and reviewing the US position on Britain's Falkland Islands claim. NATO officials say Trump cannot unilaterally expel members.

perspectives.angle: NATO pushes back at reported US threat to suspend Spain over Iran war support
perspectives.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies perceived as lacking support for the Iran war, including suspending Spain from NATO. European allies strongly rejected the notion.

headlines.headline: China's Hengli Petrochemical Denies Trading With Iran After US Sanctions
headlines.summary: Hengli Petrochemical, one of China's largest independent refiners, denied allegations from the US Treasury that it purchased Iranian oil after Washington imposed sanctions on its Dalian subsidiary. The company insists it has never traded with Iran.

headlines.headline: China unveils worlds first coal fuel cell with zero emissions
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists developed a coal fuel cell that produces electricity with zero carbon emissions, achieving higher energy efficiency than traditional burning while eliminating pollution.

headlines.headline: US lawmaker proposes bipartisan commission on political violence after WHCA shooting
headlines.summary: Rep. Ro Khanna urged creation of a bipartisan national commission to address political violence following the shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner.

headlines.headline: Italy to extradite Chinese hacker wanted by US authorities
headlines.summary: Italy has decided to extradite a Chinese national wanted by the US for hacking charges including stealing COVID-19 medical research, marking a significant win for US cybercrime enforcement.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo condemns warmongers as "thieves" on Chornobyl anniversary
headlines.summary: On the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl disaster, Pope Leo issued a stark warning about nuclear power and condemned those who wage war, calling them thieves who steal humanity's peaceful future.

headlines.headline: Americans oppose ending birthright citizenship, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
headlines.summary: A majority of Americans believe all babies born in the US should automatically receive citizenship, as the Supreme Court prepares to rule on the contentious issue.

headlines.headline: Russian Defence Minister Visits North Korea in Second High-Level Trip
headlines.summary: Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks, marking the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang in recent days. The visit underscores deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang amid the Ukraine war.

perspectives.angle: North Korea accelerates nuclear program with Russian support as Iran war erodes global security
perspectives.summary: Senior officials from Moscow and Pyongyang signal plans for long-term cooperation as North Korea capitalizes on the distraction of the Iran war to advance its nuclear ambitions, raising concerns about eroding global non-proliferation norms.

headlines.headline: Trump Says He Holds Regular Talks With Putin and Zelenskiy on Ukraine War
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump revealed he maintains direct communication with both Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he pushes for a settlement to the ongoing conflict. The disclosure comes amid intensified diplomatic efforts.

perspectives.angle: Trump Confirms Separate Calls with Putin and Zelensky on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said he has had "good conversations" with both Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky as de-escalation efforts continue.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Marks 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under War Clouds
headlines.summary: Ukraine commemorated four decades since the world's worst nuclear accident at Chornobyl, with officials warning that Russia's ongoing invasion could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Commemorates 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under Shadow of War
perspectives.summary: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster on Sunday amid ongoing fears that Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant remains a concern.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine marks Chornobyl 40th anniversary under shadow of war
perspectives.summary: Ukraine commemorated the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster amid fears that Russia four-year-old war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: China Yuan Settlements Hit Record High Amid Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: China Cross-border Interbank Payment System (CIPS) reached a record single-day transaction value of 1.22 trillion yuan, with analysts saying the Middle East conflict has acted as a catalyst for rising demand in yuan settlement, particularly in oil trade.

headlines.headline: Germany Shifts Military Strategy Away From US Dependence
headlines.summary: Germany new military doctrine emphasizes "less America, more Europe" as Berlin prepares for reduced US contributions to European security and takes greater responsibility for its own defense capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Germany Unveils First-Ever Military Strategy, Names Russia Top Threat
perspectives.summary: The Bundeswehr strategy marks Germany's first comprehensive military doctrine since WWII, explicitly identifying Russia as the primary security threat amid shifting European defense posture.

headlines.headline: Two Israeli Soldiers Killed in Jordan Border Crossing Attack
headlines.summary: Two Israeli soldiers were shot dead by a Jordanian aid truck driver near the Allenby (King Hussein) Crossing between the West Bank and Jordan. The attacker was neutralized at the site.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu orders army to attack Hezbollah in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli PM directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah in Lebanon after six people were killed by strikes despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of sabotaging Lebanon peace talks
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM says the Iran-backed group is actively undermining diplomatic efforts to reach a ceasefire agreement.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu warns Hezbollah is dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of undermining the ceasefire agreement with Lebanon, asserting Israel will continue operations against threats. IDF struck Hezbollah operatives and infrastructure in south Lebanon.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM says Hezbollah actions are threatening the ceasefire with Lebanon as sirens warn of suspected drone attacks in northern Israel.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attack on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Extension
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, just two days after a ceasefire was extended. At least six people were killed in Israeli strikes on Saturday, with cross-border exchanges straining the truce agreement.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders IDF Strikes on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Violations
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered the IDF to "vigorously" attack Hezbollah in southern Lebanon after the group launched projectiles into northern Israel. The ceasefire extension announced by Trump appears increasingly fragile as both sides exchange fire.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attacks on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Strains
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu ordered the military to vigorously attack Hezbollah targets in Lebanon after at least six people were killed in Israeli strikes. Cross-border exchanges have strained the extended ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash were not authorized
headlines.summary: Mexico's government stated that two US federal agents who died in a car crash were not permitted to participate in local operations. The agents reportedly worked for the CIA.

headlines.headline: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
headlines.summary: Ukrainian president continues diplomatic push in the Caucasus region following similar deals with Gulf states.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
perspectives.summary: President Zelensky met with Azerbaijani President Aliyev in Baku, marking his first wartime visit to Azerbaijan and signing bilateral cooperation agreements.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky Signs Cooperation Deals During First Wartime Visit to Azerbaijan
perspectives.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Azerbaijan for talks with Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev, signing cooperation agreements in his first wartime visit to the Caucasus nation.

headlines.headline: US Southern Command creates new autonomous warfare unit
headlines.summary: New command to integrate autonomous, semi-autonomous and unmanned platforms across Latin American operations.

headlines.headline: Australia foreign minister to visit Japan, China, South Korea for energy security talks
headlines.summary: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong announced visits to Japan, China and South Korea this week for discussions on energy security amid disruptions caused by the Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz tensions.

perspectives.angle: South Korea and Japan face renewed nuclear deterrent debate as Iran war reshapes security calculations
perspectives.summary: Seven in ten South Koreans now support acquiring nuclear weapons, while Japan's PM refuses to rule out the option. The Iran war has brought the long-simmering debate over nuclear proliferation in US allies to a boil, with IAEA sounding the alarm.

headlines.headline: Malian troops and Russian mercenaries withdraw from Kidal after rebel offensive
headlines.summary: Malian troops and Russian Africa Corps mercenaries have withdrawn from the northern city of Kidal following coordinated attacks by Tuareg separatists and al-Qaeda-linked JNIM militants, marking a significant shift in the Sahel conflict.

headlines.headline: France trains Ukrainian pilots on Mirage 2000-5F fighters under EU mission
headlines.summary: French Air Force pilots are training Ukrainian crews on Mirage 2000-5F aircraft within the EUMAM framework. The training includes combat operations and maintenance, marking a concrete contribution to European security.

headlines.headline: Israeli President Delays Decision on Netanyahu Pardon, Pushes for Plea Deal
headlines.summary: President Isaac Herzog said he will delay deciding on Prime Minister Netanyahus pardon request for corruption cases, urging that mediation efforts be exhausted first. The decision comes amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

perspectives.angle: Israel President Herzog seeks plea deal over pardon for Netanyahu
perspectives.summary: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's pardon request, instead pushing for mediation that could lead to a plea deal in his corruption trial. Netanyahu faces charges in three cases.

headlines.headline: Russia warns months needed to rebalance oil market after Hormuz blockade
headlines.summary: Russian Deputy PM Alexander Novak warned of deep energy crisis as the Hormuz blockade prevents large volumes of oil from reaching global markets.

headlines.headline: China positioned to benefit from energy-driven renewables race
headlines.summary: The US-Iran war may have inadvertently triggered a global energy transition favoring Beijing's green technology leadership, analysts say.

headlines.headline: Car bomb explodes outside Belfast police station in suspected dissident attack
headlines.summary: A car bomb exploded outside a police station in Dunmurry, south Belfast, after a delivery vehicle was hijacked. Northern Ireland police called it a "miracle" no one was injured and suspect dissident republicans, possibly the New IRA.

perspectives.angle: Car Bomb Explodes Outside Northern Ireland Police Station
perspectives.summary: A car bomb attack on a police station in Dunmurry, near Belfast, has been condemned as an attempt to undermine the 1998 Good Friday Agreement. Dissident Republican groups are suspected.

headlines.headline: Australia announces M armored vehicle deal amid weapons buildup
headlines.summary: Australia will spend more than $800 million on armored vehicles, adding to its recent weapons purchases as the Middle East conflict drives concerns about regional security and supply chain vulnerabilities.

headlines.headline: Iran FM Araqchi discusses Hormuz security and war end efforts with Oman
headlines.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi held talks in Oman on Sunday to discuss security in the Strait of Hormuz and broader Gulf waters, as well as diplomatic efforts to end the Iran-U.S. conflict. The meeting comes as US-Iran peace talks remain stalled.

headlines.headline: King Charles state visit under security review after Washington shooting
headlines.summary: Buckingham Palace is holding discussions with US authorities about whether the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner will affect security planning for King Charles's state visit to the US this week. The King expressed relief that Trump was unharmed.

headlines.headline: China backs Myanmar's push to ease diplomatic isolation, fight telecoms scams
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi concluded a five-day Southeast Asia tour by backing Myanmar's efforts to reduce its diplomatic isolation and crack down on telecoms fraud operations.

headlines.headline: Two US citizens among 19 killed in Philippines clashes with communist rebels
headlines.summary: Two American citizens were among 19 suspected New People's Army guerrillas killed during clashes with Philippine military forces on April 19 in Negros Occidental province.

headlines.headline: EU commissioners arrive in Kyiv to secure Chornobyl site
headlines.summary: EU Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis and Energy Commissioner Dan Jørgensen visited Kyiv on April 26 to discuss making the Chornobyl nuclear site safe again, marking continued European support for Ukraine's nuclear safety.

headlines.headline: Syria holds first public trial of Assad-era officials in Damascus
headlines.summary: Syria opened its first public trial of officials linked to former President Bashar Assad's rule in Damascus on Sunday, marking a significant step in post-civil war accountability efforts.

headlines.headline: Mexico: US agents killed in crash not authorized for local operations
headlines.summary: Two US federal agents, reportedly CIA, died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab. Mexican authorities say they were not permitted to operate in the country.

perspectives.angle: Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Weren't Authorized to Operate There
perspectives.summary: The Mexican government stated that two US federal agents - widely reported to be CIA officers - killed in a car crash during an anti-narcotics raid were not authorized to operate on Mexican territory. One had entered as a visitor, the other on a diplomatic passport.

headlines.headline: Palestinians hold local elections in West Bank and Gaza
headlines.summary: Local elections conducted in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups did not participate.

perspectives.angle: Palestinians vote in local elections across West Bank and Gaza
perspectives.summary: Local elections held in the occupied West Bank and one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not participating in what marks a rare electoral moment.

perspectives.angle: Gaza to hold first local election in two decades
perspectives.summary: Residents of Deir al-Balah are set to vote in municipal elections, the first in Gaza in 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the elections scheduled for this weekend.

perspectives.angle: Gaza Set for First Local Elections in Two Decades
perspectives.summary: Gaza is preparing to hold its first local elections in nearly 20 years. Hamas is not participating in the municipal elections in Deir al-Balah scheduled for this weekend.

headlines.headline: Afghanistan urges Afghans stuck in Qatar to return home
headlines.summary: Taliban government calls on Afghans who helped US forces and are now stranded in Qatar to return to Afghanistan.

headlines.headline: Venezuela prisoner release scheme nearing end, rights groups warn
headlines.summary: More than 500 political prisoners remain jailed as amnesty program winds down amid criticism from human rights organizations.

headlines.headline: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Campaign Targeting Politicians
headlines.summary: The German government believes Russia orchestrated phishing attacks via the Signal messaging app targeting high-profile politicians, diplomats, military officers, and journalists. At least 300 accounts were compromised in the cyber campaign.

perspectives.angle: Germany attributes global Signal and WhatsApp cyber campaign to Russia
perspectives.summary: Berlin says the coordinated disinformation attack on encrypted messaging platforms likely originated in Moscow.

perspectives.angle: Germany Blames Russia for Global Cyber Campaign Targeting Signal and WhatsApp
perspectives.summary: Berlin believes Russia is behind a global cyber operation targeting encrypted messaging apps Signal and WhatsApp, according to Der Spiegel, marking an escalation in Russian cyber warfare tactics.

perspectives.angle: Germany attributes global Signal and WhatsApp cyber campaign to Russia
perspectives.summary: Berlin believes the coordinated attack on encrypted messaging platforms presumably originated in Russia, according to Der Spiegel, highlighting ongoing hybrid warfare concerns.

headlines.headline: Colombia Bus Bombing Kills 13 Ahead of Presidential Election
headlines.summary: At least 13 people were killed and 17 wounded in an explosives attack on a bus in western Colombia. Authorities blamed dissidents of the FARC guerrilla group for the violence amid escalating pre-election tensions.

perspectives.angle: Colombia bus bombing kills 13 amid escalating drug-trafficking violence
perspectives.summary: An explosive device detonated on a bus in Cauca province, killing 13 and injuring 38 including five children. Army chief called it a terrorist act blamed on FARC dissidents, amid a wave of 26 similar incidents in the last two days.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 Ahead of Presidential Election
perspectives.summary: A bomb attack on the Pan-American Highway in Cauca department killed 14 and injured 38, including five minors. Authorities blamed FARC dissidents led by Ivan Mordisco, the country's most-wanted criminal, as political violence escalates before the May 31 vote.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Highway Bombing Kills 14 as President Blames Rebel Dissidents
perspectives.summary: A bombing on a Colombian highway killed at least 14 people. President Gustavo Petro blamed the attack on rebels linked to dissident FARC factions. The attacks come one month before the presidential election.

headlines.headline: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott Over Russian Athlete Decision
headlines.summary: Finland announced it will not host international championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in protest against the governing body's policy.

perspectives.angle: Finland Joins Nordic Boycott of World Aquatics Over Russia Participation
perspectives.summary: Finland announced it will not host international swimming championships while World Aquatics allows Russian and Belarusian athletes to compete, joining Norway and Poland in a growing Nordic protest against the governing body.

headlines.headline: France and Greece Strengthen Defense Alliance Amid NATO Uncertainty
headlines.summary: French President Macron and Greek PM Mitsotakis signed expanded bilateral defense agreements in Athens, affirming that EU defense capabilities complement rather than replace NATO. The Greece-France Alliance 2.0 comes as concerns grow over US commitment to the alliance.

perspectives.angle: EU leaders prepare mutual defense blueprint amid Trump NATO withdrawal threats
perspectives.summary: European Commission will draft a plan to use the EUs Article 42.7 mutual assistance clause if a member is attacked, as Trump intensifies criticism of NATO and considers suspending Spain from the alliance over its refusal to join the Iran war.

perspectives.angle: EU leaders activate mutual defence pact amid Trump NATO threats
perspectives.summary: European leaders are looking to a little-known mutual assistance clause in EU treaties as Donald Trump threatens NATO allies. The move reflects growing concerns about US commitment to European security.

headlines.headline: Russian Defense Minister Visits North Korea as Military Ties Deepen
headlines.summary: Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks with top leadership, the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang this week. The visit follows State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin meeting with Kim Jong Un to commemorate North Korean troop deployment in Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: North Korea and Russia Deepen Military Ties, Open Memorial for Korean War Dead
perspectives.summary: North Korea and Russia pledged deeper military cooperation as they opened a memorial for North Korean troops killed fighting in Ukraine. The two countries are building what they call a "powerful bulwark" against Western influence as the Ukraine war continues.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Opens Memorial Museum for Troops Killed in Ukraine War
perspectives.summary: North Korea inaugurated a memorial museum in Pyongyang honoring soldiers killed fighting for Russia, with Kim Jong Un attending alongside Russian officials including Defense Minister Andrei Beloushov. Russia and North Korea agreed to long-term military cooperation covering 2027-2031.

headlines.headline: TotalEnergies Warns of Global Energy Shortages as Hormuz Crisis Continues
headlines.summary: The head of TotalEnergies warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months, as French President Macron reaffirmed efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. LNG markets are bracing for sustained price elevation throughout 2026.

perspectives.angle: Global LNG Markets Face Sustained Price Elevation as Hormuz Closure Continues
perspectives.summary: Global liquefied natural gas markets are bracing for prolonged price elevation throughout 2026 as the Strait of Hormuz closure continues to throttle supply, with analysts warning short-term shocks risk becoming structural and permanently altering demand patterns.

headlines.headline: Chinese analysts study how Iran detected and damaged US F-35 stealth jet
headlines.summary: South China Morning Post reports that Chinese military analysts are mapping out how Iran managed to detect and damage a US F-35 stealth fighter during combat operations, suggesting electro-optical/infrared sensor systems may have been used.

headlines.headline: Dutch intelligence: Russia could fight NATO within a year after Ukraine war ends
headlines.summary: The Dutch MIVD warns Russia could be ready for a regional conflict with NATO within a year after hostilities in Ukraine cease, aiming to create political division in the alliance through limited territorial gains under nuclear threats.

headlines.headline: Thailand and Cambodia sign ceasefire with Trump presiding at ASEAN summit
headlines.summary: The two nations signed an expanded ceasefire deal ending their five-day war, with Trump taking credit for pushing negotiations. Thailand will release prisoners and Cambodia will withdraw heavy artillery as first steps.

headlines.headline: Putin Approval Ratings Drop as Russian Influencers Voice Growing Discontent
headlines.summary: A viral 19-minute video by a Russian blogger with 31 million views has sparked a wave of public criticism over internet restrictions and the strained wartime economy. Putin's approval fell to 65.6% in state polls, the lowest since before the Ukraine war began, amid growing frustration with the conflict's fifth year.

headlines.headline: Japanese Destroyer Transits Taiwan Strait in First Major Passage Under Takaichi
headlines.summary: The JS Ikazuchi, a Murasame-class destroyer, conducted a deliberate 14-hour transit through the Taiwan Strait, sparking outrage in Beijing. The move signals a significant shift in Japan's regional security stance under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

headlines.headline: Macron Warns Europe Must Wake Up as US, Russia, and China Align Against It
headlines.summary: Speaking in Athens, the French president called for European strategic autonomy, saying Washington, Moscow, and Beijing are now categorically opposed to European interests. The remarks come as Germany unveils its first comprehensive defense strategy since WWII.

headlines.headline: Macron Affirms EU Defense Clause Stronger Than NATO's Article 5
headlines.summary: The French president said during a visit to Greece that the EU's mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is 'stronger' than NATO's collective defense provision, citing military aid sent to Cyprus after a drone attack. The statement comes amid uncertainty over US commitment to NATO.

perspectives.angle: Macron: EU Joint Defense Clause Is Stronger Than NATOs Article 5
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is steadier than NATOs Article 5, pushing for greater European strategic autonomy amid questions about US reliability.

perspectives.angle: Macron says EU defence clause stronger than NATO Article 5
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the EU mutual assistance clause was "stronger" than NATO Article 5, citing recent military aid to Cyprus as proof of Europe ability to defend itself amid concerns over US reliability.

perspectives.angle: Macron says EU defense clause is stronger than NATO Article 5
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron declared during a visit to Greece that the EU mutual defense clause (Article 42.7) is stronger than NATO Article 5 because it leaves no option for non-compliance. The statement comes as Trump questions NATO and US commitment to European defense.

headlines.headline: Ukrainians Mark Chernobyl Anniversary Amid War Fears
headlines.summary: Residents of Slavutych gathered for a midnight vigil commemorating the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear disaster, defying wartime curfews. The anniversary comes as Ukraine worries Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: Tunisia suspends one of Africas oldest human rights groups in widening crackdown
headlines.summary: Authorities ordered a one-month suspension of the Tunisian League for Human Rights, part of the Nobel Peace Prize-winning National Dialogue Quartet. The move signals deepening authoritarianism in the North African nation.

headlines.headline: Chinas top diplomat meets Myanmar junta leader on regional tour
headlines.summary: Chinas Foreign Minister visited Naypyidaw and met with the military-backed government leader, deepening Beijings regional ties as Myanmars civil war continues. The visit underscores Chinas growing influence in Southeast Asia.

perspectives.angle: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi Meets Myanmar's Military Leader
perspectives.summary: China's top diplomat visited Myanmar's capital Naypyitaw and met with Min Aung Hlaing as part of a regional tour aimed at strengthening Beijing's political, security and strategic ties in Southeast Asia. The visit follows Min Aung Hlaing's swearing in as president on April 10.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels Kushner and Witkoff Iran Mission After Tehran Offer Falls Short
headlines.summary: President Trump cancelled his envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, saying Tehran 'offered a lot but not enough.' Iran had said no direct talks were planned and its foreign minister left Islamabad for Oman before heading to Russia.

perspectives.angle: Trump Tells Iran to "Call Us" as Putin Hosts Tehran Top Diplomat in Russia
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump publicly urges Iran to negotiate as Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi meets with Vladimir Putin in St. Petersburg following canceled US envoy talks.

headlines.headline: Republicans Recalibrate Midterm Strategy Amid Trump Approval Slump and Iran War
headlines.summary: With gas prices up, Trump's approval ratings down, and the Iran war dragging on, Republicans are retooling their midterm election blueprint to focus on Trump's policies while distancing themselves from the president personally.

perspectives.angle: Iran war deadline triggers Trump-Congress showdown
perspectives.summary: The 60-day limit under the War Powers Resolution for military operations without congressional approval expires May 1, forcing Trump to seek authorization or find alternative ways to legitimize the Iran operation as diplomatic efforts continue.

headlines.headline: Hungary's Orban Refuses Parliament Seat After Historic Election Defeat
headlines.summary: Viktor Orban, Hungary's dominant political figure for 36 years, said he will not take up his parliament seat following his party's landslide defeat. He plans to lead Fidesz through a "renewal" process, with analysts seeing his loss as evidence that right-wing populism may be peaking.

perspectives.angle: Hungary's Orban to Leave Parliament After Landslide Election Defeat
perspectives.summary: Viktor Orban announced he will give up his parliamentary mandate after losing to Peter Magyar in Hungary's election. The long-serving prime minister said he wants to stay on as Fidesz party leader while rebuilding the national side.

headlines.headline: India Signs $1.2 Billion Deal for Russian Long-Range Air-to-Air Missiles
headlines.summary: India has reportedly signed a deal for 300 Russian R-37M ultra-long-range missiles designed to target support aircraft like airborne early-warning and tanker planes. The move comes as Pakistan and China continue integrating their aerial weapons systems.

headlines.headline: Trump Fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem
headlines.summary: President Trump fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem following mounting criticism over her handling of immigration enforcement and disaster response. Senator Markwayne Mullin has been nominated as her replacement.

headlines.headline: Acid Attacks Target Indonesian Activists Amid Growing Climate of Repression
headlines.summary: Environmental and human rights activists in Indonesia face escalating attacks, including acid assaults, under President Prabowo's government which rights groups say is increasingly hostile to dissent.

headlines.headline: EU Seeks Alternative Middle East Energy Routes to Bypass Hormuz
headlines.summary: European Commission President von der Leyen announces plans to work with Gulf countries on new export infrastructure that avoids the Strait of Hormuz, as the bloc's energy bill has skyrocketed by €25 billion due to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Former MIT President Warns US Is Losing Innovation Race to China
headlines.summary: L. Rafael Reif, who led MIT from 2012 to 2022, argues in Foreign Affairs that America is ceding scientific leadership to China, pointing to advances in EVs, nuclear energy, and military technology.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Civilians Question Fragile Ceasefire as Violence Simmers in South
headlines.summary: Residents in southern Lebanon express skepticism about the extended ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, as continuing attacks and tensions threaten to reignite the conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany Readies Naval Units for Possible Hormuz Deployment
headlines.summary: Germany is sending naval units to the Mediterranean in preparation for possible deployment in an international mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which remains blocked by Iran.

perspectives.angle: Germany deploys minesweeper to Mediterranean for possible Strait of Hormuz mission
perspectives.summary: Germany's defense minister announced the deployment of a minesweeper to the Mediterranean Sea in preparation for a potential mission to the Strait of Hormuz, as European nations consider naval operations to secure the vital oil transit route.

perspectives.angle: Germany Deploying Warships to Mediterranean for Possible Hormuz Mission
perspectives.summary: German navy vessels are being sent to the Mediterranean as Berlin considers a potential role in protecting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran attacks on commercial vessels.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy Signs Military-Industrial Agreement With Azerbaijan as Russian Attacks Kill 10
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president signed a military-industrial cooperation agreement in Baku as Russia continued missile and drone strikes on Ukrainian cities. The death toll from recent attacks rose to at least 10.

headlines.headline: Washingtons Resentment Toward Paper Tiger NATO Growing
headlines.summary: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity during the Iran war, with frustration particularly strong toward Spain, France, and the United Kingdom over their perceived lack of support.

headlines.headline: Japans Accelerated Remilitarization Raises Regional Concerns
headlines.summary: Japans quickened remilitarization under the Takaichi administration, including eased restrictions on exporting lethal weapons, is posing grave threats to neighbors and requires international vigilance, analysts warn.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Exports Drone Warfare Expertise to Allies
headlines.summary: Ukraines hard-won drone warfare knowledge is being shared with allies, as three Ukrainian drone operators reveal how the country has become a global leader in unmanned aerial warfare technology.

headlines.headline: Colombia explosives attack kills at least 13
headlines.summary: An explosives attack in western Colombia killed at least 13 people and wounded 17 others, in one of the deadliest incidents amid escalating violence by FARC dissident groups.

perspectives.angle: Colombia Highway Bomb Attack Kills 10 Ahead of Presidential Election
perspectives.summary: A highway bombing in the Cauca department killed 10 people and injured at least 12, with authorities blaming FARC dissidents. The attack comes amid a wave of violence before next month's presidential election.

headlines.headline: Russia parliament speaker visits Pyongyang for war memorial ceremony
headlines.summary: Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin arrived in North Korea to attend the opening of a memorial for North Korean troops who died fighting in Ukraine, highlighting deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: China and Mozambique to map critical minerals in conflict zone
headlines.summary: China and Mozambique agreed to survey rare earth elements, graphite, and lithium deposits in insurgency-hit Cabo Delgado province, as Beijing seeks to secure critical mineral supplies for the green energy transition.

headlines.headline: Iran Resumes Commercial Flights From Tehran Airport
headlines.summary: Iran has resumed commercial flights from Tehran Imam Khomeini International Airport for the first time since the US-Israel conflict began two months ago, with flights operating to Istanbul, Muscat, Medina, and domestic destinations including Mashhad.

headlines.headline: Macron warns US not "fully reliable ally," urges European self-reliance
headlines.summary: French President Macron said the US can no longer be seen as a fully reliable ally during a visit to Athens, calling for greater European autonomy in defense. The remarks came after a Pentagon memo revealed US plans to suspend Spain's NATO membership over its refusal to support the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia looks to China and regional partners as US security guarantee wavers
headlines.summary: The Iran war has exposed vulnerabilities in US security guarantees to Gulf allies, prompting Saudi Arabia to diversify partnerships with China, Pakistan, and Turkey. Riyadh has signed defense deals with Beijing and a mutual defense pact with nuclear-armed Pakistan.

headlines.headline: China Warns US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Global Chip Supply Chains
headlines.summary: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, warning of potential global supply chain disruptions.

headlines.headline: Mexico Says US Agents Killed in Crash Were Not Authorized for Local Operations
headlines.summary: Mexicos government stated that two US federal agents killed in a car crash in the northern region were not authorized to participate in any local operation.

headlines.headline: China Drops Hint Its Next Aircraft Carrier May Be Nuclear-Powered
headlines.summary: A video released for the PLA Navys anniversary uses wordplay suggesting a new nuclear-powered warship is under construction, potentially reshaping the Pacific naval balance.

headlines.headline: Trump Renews Call to End Filibuster Over SAVE America Act Impasse
headlines.summary: President Trump called for eliminating the filibuster to pass the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act, which remains stalled in the Senate. The push highlights ongoing legislative gridlock on election-related legislation.

headlines.headline: US eases sanctions to let Venezuela pay Maduro legal fees
headlines.summary: The US Department of Justice modified sanctions to allow the Venezuelan government to cover legal fees for former president Nicolas Maduro, who faces federal drug trafficking charges in New York. Maduro defense lawyers had argued that blocking payment violated his right to counsel.

headlines.headline: US senator urges China participation in nuclear arms control
headlines.summary: Senator Deb Fischer, chair of the Senate Armed Services strategic forces subcommittee, said China must be involved in any nuclear arms control talks. China has repeatedly refused to join US-Russia nuclear agreements, seeking parity before negotiations.

headlines.headline: Gaza's Deir el-Balah Holds First Municipal Elections in 20 Years
headlines.summary: Residents of Deir el-Balah in central Gaza cast ballots in the first municipal elections since 2006, with voters expressing joy at participating in democratic processes after two decades. The elections come amid stalled ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Islamabad bears the cost of Pakistan mediates US-Iran negotiations
headlines.summary: Pakistani capital experiences semi-lockdown conditions as the country attempts to broker peace between Washington and Tehran. Next round of talks remains uncertain as daily life disrupted.

perspectives.angle: Iran Foreign Minister Heads to Russia After Pakistan Talks Amid Stalled US Negotiations
perspectives.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi left Pakistan for Russia after meeting Pakistani officials to discuss ending the war with the US, as Tehran continues diplomatic efforts despite Trump canceling his envoys planned trip to Islamabad.

headlines.headline: China tungsten export restrictions hit global hobby and defense supply chains
headlines.summary: Beijing limits exports of tungsten, a critical material for both hobby manufacturing and defense industries. The restrictions come amid broader trade tensions and supply chain realignments.

headlines.headline: Low Earth orbit control could determine future world order, Chinese analysis warns
headlines.summary: A Chinese strategic analysis highlights Starlink capabilities in conflict zones and warns of a new form of power that could allow or cut connectivity in real time, reshaping geopolitical leverage.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia Swap 193 Prisoners Each in UAE-Facilitated Exchange
headlines.summary: Russia and Ukraine exchanged 193 prisoners of war each in a swap facilitated by the United States and UAE. The returned prisoners included soldiers, border guards, and police, some with injuries.

perspectives.angle: Russia-Ukraine Exchange Deadly Attacks as Zelenskyy Calls for More Talks
perspectives.summary: Russian drone and missile strikes killed at least 3 and wounded 21 in Dnipro overnight. Both sides reported casualties in intensified fighting as Zelenskyy pushed for continued diplomatic engagement.

headlines.headline: HH-60W Jolly Green II helicopters to take on doomsday evacuation role in Washington
headlines.summary: The US Air Force will modify its new combat rescue helicopters for VIP airlift and continuity of government operations, replacing aging UH-1N Twin Hueys.

headlines.headline: Navy rushing Hellfire missiles to carrier strike groups for counter-drone ops
headlines.summary: The US Navy is pushing more hard-kill counter-drone capabilities to its fleet as it confronts growing threats from Iranian one-way attack drones in the Middle East.

perspectives.angle: Navy Rushing Hellfire Missiles to Carrier Strike Groups for Drone Defense
